scenario: A booking form requires the user to pick a future delivery date. The form should display a calendar picker, restrict selection to today or later, output an ISO 8601 date string.
question: Which Forms component + configuration fits?

options.text: Datetime component with Subtype = Date and Disallow past dates enabled. The Camunda Forms Datetime element covers Date, Time, and Date-and-Time input via its Subtype property. Setting Subtype to Date renders a calendar picker. Enabling Disallow past dates prevents selecting any date before today without requiring custom FEEL validation. The output is a YYYY-MM-DD ISO 8601 string stored in the bound process variable.

Textfield component with a regex validation pattern constraining input to YYYY-MM-DD format. A Textfield presents a plain text input without any calendar picker UI. Users must type the date string manually without visual date navigation, month selection, or weekday labeling. This produces poor user experience for date entry and does not prevent semantically invalid dates that match the format pattern but represent impossible calendar days.

Number Input component bound to a date variable. Number components accept numeric values such as integers and decimals and store them as numbers. Date values -- which are ISO 8601 strings in YYYY-MM-DD format -- are fundamentally incompatible with a numeric input component. There is no way to represent a calendar date as a number in a way that supports calendar pickers, future-date restriction, or ISO 8601 string output.

Camunda 8 Forms has no dedicated date picker component, requiring workarounds for calendar date input. This is factually incorrect. The Camunda Forms element library includes the Datetime component specifically designed for date and time input. With Subtype set to Date, it renders a calendar picker UI. It supports future-date restriction via Disallow past dates and outputs ISO 8601 date strings, fully covering the booking form requirement.

a.text: Correct. The canonical date input in Camunda Forms 8.8 is the Datetime component (per forms-element-library-datetime.md:3-9). Its Subtype property (:17) accepts Date, Time, or Date & Time. For "today or later", set the dedicated Disallow past dates boolean property (:25 -- "Enables the restriction to not allow past dates"); no FEEL validate.min or =today() config is needed (validation block exposes only Required per :26-27). When Subtype = Date, the output datatype is an ISO 8601 string YYYY-MM-DD (:33).

b.text: Incorrect. Textfield + regex provides no calendar picker; users must hand-type the date.

d.text: Incorrect. Datetime component covers all date use cases.

For "today or later", set the dedicated Disallow past dates boolean property (:25 -- "Enables the restriction to not allow past dates"); no FEEL validate.min or =today() config is needed (validation block exposes only Required per :26-27). When Subtype = Date, the output datatype is an ISO 8601 string YYYY-MM-DD (:33).
